title: Pest controllers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in landscaping or groundskeeping activities. Work may involve reviewing contracts to ascertain service, machine, and workforce requirements; answering inquiries from potential customers regarding methods, material, and price ranges; and preparing estimates according to labor, material, and machine costs. Apply or release chemical solutions or toxic gases and set traps to kill or remove pests and vermin that infest buildings and surrounding areas. Mix or apply pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or insecticides through sprays, dusts, vapors, soil incorporation, or chemical application on trees, shrubs, lawns, or crops. Usually requires specific training and state or federal certification.

tasks.task: Record work activities performed.
Inspect premises to identify infestation source and extent of damage to property, wall, roof porosity, and access to infested locations.
Measure area dimensions requiring treatment, calculate fumigant requirements, and prepare service estimates based on labor, material, and machine costs.
Recommend treatment and prevention methods for pest problems to clients. Answer inquiries from current or prospective customers regarding methods, materials, or price ranges.

Mix pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or insecticides for application to trees, shrubs, lawns, or crops. Fill sprayer tanks with water and chemicals, connect hoses and nozzles, and operate machinery to apply chemical solutions, powders, or gases safely.

Start motors and engage machinery, such as sprayer agitators or pumps, to direct spray over designated areas, applying knowledge of weather conditions, droplet sizes, and environmental factors for effective pest control.
Identify lawn or plant diseases to determine appropriate treatment. Investigate work-related complaints to verify issues and recommend resolutions.
Direct, or assist other workers in pesticide application processes and treatment or extermination procedures to control pests and vermin.
Tour grounds to inspect conditions of plants and soil, maintaining required records such as project and personnel reports.
Schedule work for crews based on work priorities, and establish and enforce operating procedures to ensure personnel safety and performance standards.
Train workers in pest control tasks, provide assistance as needed to meet deadlines, and supervise the installation of systems for pest management, as well as landscaping activities.
Investigation of infested areas and study preliminary reports to determine the required treatment type and maintain budgets for individual projects.
Clean or service machinery to ensure operational efficiency, and investigate issues related to equipment used in pest control operations.
Confer with managers or relevant stakeholders to coordinate work activities and ensure compliance with specifications and contract requirements.
Inventory supplies of tools, equipment, and materials to ensure sufficiency and usability for pest control operations.
Install or maintain landscaped areas, including performing necessary maintenance and repairs.
Perform administrative duties, such as authorizing leaves, and engage in personnel-related activities, including hiring and evaluating staff performance.
Set mechanical traps or place harmful bait in designated areas as part of pest control strategies.
Post warning signs and secure areas to be fumigated to ensure safety and compliance.
Provide driving instructions to ensure complete coverage of designated treatment areas and manage driving of equipment like trucks and sprayers.
Design or supervise the installation of systems for pest management, such as sprinkler systems.
Recommend changes in working conditions or equipment used to increase crew efficiency and safety.

emerging_tasks.task: Establish driving routes for pesticide applications.
Record information about pesticide applications, such as the type used and amount applied.
Use new technology and equipment, such as drones or GPS systems, to apply pesticides more accurately and efficiently.

alternate_titles: Pest Control Route Technician (Pest Control Route Tech)
